cards.front: `ip host` command
cards.back: Statically map a host name (or FQDN) to an IP address on a Cisco device.

cards.front: Statically map a host name (or FQDN) to an IP address on a Cisco device.
cards.back: `ip host` command

cards.front: `ip dns server` command
cards.back: Configures the Cisco device itself to act as a DNS server.

cards.front: Configures the Cisco device itself to act as a DNS server.
cards.back: `ip dns server` command

cards.front: `ip name-server <ip>` command
cards.back: Configures the Cisco device to use another device for DNS name resolution.

cards.front: Configures the Cisco device to use another device for DNS name resolution.
cards.back: `ip name-server <ip>` command

cards.front: Two commands to fix Router1 so www and www.example.com resolve to 198.51.100.10?
cards.back:
```
ip host www 198.51.100.10
ip host www.example.com 198.51.100.10
```

cards.front: How do you verify a Cisco device is acting as the DNS server?
cards.back: `show running-config` — look for `ip dns server` (acts as server) or `ip name-server <addr>` (points at another).

cards.front: In the sim, every device points at [...] as its DNS server (Router1's Serial 1/0 IP).
To edit one statement in a named ACL without breaking sequence/identifiers, you must
cards.back: Enter `ip access-list extended <name>` mode, `no <seq>` the bad statement, then re-add the corrected statement using the same `<seq>` number.

cards.front: Enter `ip access-list extended <name>` mode, `no <seq>` the bad statement, then re-add the corrected statement using the same `<seq>` number.
cards.back: To edit one statement in a named ACL without breaking sequence/identifiers, you must

cards.front: Command to enter named ACL config mode for ACL `webftp`?
cards.back: `ip access-list extended webftp`

cards.front: Inside named ACL mode, how do you remove statement 20 then replace it?
Why not just `no ip access-list extended webftp` + rebuild?
cards.back: Destroys ALL statements + breaks the `ip access-group webftp in` binding mid-edit. Sim rule: "no changes other than required, do not modify sequencing or identifiers."

cards.front: Wildcard mask
cards.back: Reverse subnet mask — bits set to 0 must match exactly, bits set to 1 are ignored. Calculate: 255.255.255.255 minus the subnet mask.

cards.front: Reverse subnet mask — bits set to 0 must match exactly, bits set to 1 are ignored. Calculate: 255.255.255.255 minus the subnet mask.
cards.back: Wildcard mask

cards.front: Wildcard `0.0.0.63` = subnet mask [...] = /26 = 64 addresses (one VLAN).
Wildcard `0.0.0.63` = subnet mask [...] = /26 = 64 addresses (one VLAN).
Wildcard `0.0.0.63` = subnet mask [...] = /26 = 64 addresses (one VLAN).
Wildcard `0.0.0.255` = subnet mask [...] = /24 = full Class C (all 3 VLANs in 192.0.2.0/24).
Wildcard `0.0.0.255` = subnet mask [...] = /24 = full Class C (all 3 VLANs in 192.0.2.0/24).
Wildcard `0.0.1.255` = subnet mask [...] = /23 = supernet of 192.0.2.0 + 192.0.3.0 (too wide for sim — leaks beyond intended VLANs).
Wildcard `0.0.1.255` = subnet mask [...] = /23 = supernet of 192.0.2.0 + 192.0.3.0 (too wide for sim — leaks beyond intended VLANs).
Why was webftp stmt 20 (`permit tcp 192.0.2.0 0.0.0.63 host 198.51.100.10 eq www`) wrong?
cards.back: Wildcard 0.0.0.63 = only VLAN 100 (Web). Task says ALL Switch2 VLANs need HTTP to www. Fix: widen to `0.0.0.255` (full /24).

cards.front: Why was webftp stmt 30 (`permit tcp 192.0.2.0 0.0.1.255 host 198.51.100.11 eq ftp`) wrong?
cards.back: Wildcard 0.0.1.255 = /23 supernet, allows all 3 VLANs + adjacent net. Task says ONLY Web dept (VLAN 100) gets FTP. Fix: narrow to `0.0.0.63` (VLAN 100 only).

cards.front: ACL keyword `www` matches TCP port [...] (HTTP).
ACL keyword `ftp` matches TCP port [...] (FTP control port — only port needed for passive FTP).
ACL value `53` in `eq 53` matches the [...] port. UDP 53 for queries; TCP 53 for zone transfers / packets >512 bytes.
cards.back: DNS

cards.front: Why does passive FTP only need port 21 permitted (not 20)?
cards.back: Passive FTP: client opens both control (21) AND data connections outbound. Server's port 20 isn't used for data — server tells client an ephemeral port over the port-21 control channel. Permitting `eq ftp` (21) is enough.

cards.front: `show ip interface` on Router2 reveals it has 4 [...] (fa0/0, fa0/0.100, fa0/0.200, fa0/0.300), each /26, each with `Inbound access list is webftp`.
cards.back: FastEthernet 0/0 subinterfaces

cards.front: VLAN ↔ subnet ↔ subinterface mapping in sim?
How do you match Switch2 ports to VLANs?
cards.back: `show vlan` on Switch2. In sim: fa0/3 → VLAN 100, fa0/6 → VLAN 200, fa0/10 → VLAN 300.

cards.front: Apply ACL inbound on interface
cards.back: `ip access-group <name> in` under interface config mode.

cards.front: `ip access-group <name> in` under interface config mode.
cards.back: Apply ACL inbound on interface

cards.front: Why apply ACL inbound (vs outbound)?
cards.back: Inbound = ACL evaluated BEFORE routing decision. Closer to source, drops bad traffic earlier, less router CPU. Outbound = packet already routed before drop.

cards.front: ACL implicit deny rule
cards.back: Any packet not matching a `permit` statement is dropped. Always at least one `permit` needed or ACL blocks everything.

cards.front: Any packet not matching a `permit` statement is dropped. Always at least one `permit` needed or ACL blocks everything.
cards.back: ACL implicit deny rule

cards.front: Router1 fix (DNS):
cards.back:
```
Router1>enable
Router1#configure terminal
Router1(config)#ip host www 198.51.100.10
Router1(config)#ip host www.example.com 198.51.100.10
```

cards.front: Router2 fix (webftp ACL stmt 20 widen, stmt 30 narrow):
cards.back:
```
Router2>enable
Router2#configure terminal
Router2(config)#ip access-list extended webftp
Router2(config-ext-nacl)#no 20
Router2(config-ext-nacl)#20 permit tcp 192.0.2.0 0.0.0.255 host 198.51.100.10 eq www
Router2(config-ext-nacl)#no 30
Router2(config-ext-nacl)#30 permit tcp 192.0.2.0 0.0.0.63 host 198.51.100.11 eq ftp
```

cards.front: Persist config across reboot
cards.back: `copy running-config startup-config`

cards.front:
Sim symptom → diagnostic command:
- "Can't reach by host name, can by IP" → [...] on DNS server (Router1) → check `ip host` entries
- "Some VLANs can HTTP, others can't" → `show ip access-lists` on Router2 → check wildcards per stmt
- "Which interface has ACL applied?" → `show ip interface` → look for `Inbound/Outbound access list is <name>`
- "Which switch port is in which VLAN?" → `show vlan` on switch
cards.back: `show running-config`

cards.front: Sim discipline rule (do not violate)?
cards.back: Make ONLY required changes. Do NOT add new ACL statements. Do NOT change ACL sequence numbers or identifiers. Use `no <seq>` + re-add with same `<seq>` to edit in place.
